Text: Over the course of our professional and interpersonal relationship, Vrijen has demonstrated a plenitude of qualities that explain not only his personal success in the roles he took on, but also his contributions to the success of the organizations in which he worked. Vrijen’s intelligence, dedication, expertise, and collegiality have made him both a pleasure to work with and a trusted professional connection. I would not hesitate to recommend Vrijen to any organization that seeks to cultivate a culture of success.  When I first met Vrijen at Prep Zone, he established himself quickly and learned new things with an intensity I have rarely seen. He quickly rose through the ranks and, although a fresh grad, deservedly became Head of Business Development for our consulting division within months of joining. He showed the relevant competencies of a successful educational consultant and driver of business: creativity, smarts, personability, teamwork. He was a huge asset to our fast-paced consulting team, contributing to its 300%+ margins even as our department’s two most experienced colleagues left the company.  I later moved jobs and was approached by a recruiter who was looking for appropriate candidates to help lead the establishment of a new multimillion dollar educational company in Singapore called the Keys. Having just made a move, I declined, but I immediately recommended Vrijen for the position. Based on my experience working alongside him, I knew he would be able to thrive in a dynamic and high-stakes environment. He proved my instincts correct by helping his division in the Keys to become the company’s most successful by far.  Outside of these corporate interactions, I have known Vrijen as someone whom I can turn to for professional advice of any kind: product, marketing, sales, or otherwise. His greatest, and rarest, assets are his resourcefulness, creativity, and charisma, but he has also acquired a great deal of expertise in the educational industry in a short amount of time. He has since shifted his focus to public relations, a field which I think fits him like a glove. He has the inherent qualities to make him highly successful in the PR world, and I am certain he is learning the less instinctive elements of the profession quickly in his master’s program.  Above all, Vrijen makes others around him better than they would otherwise be. His easy charm, his good nature, his empathy, and his ingenuity are as infectious as they are uplifting. Having a team member like Vrijen, who makes a job pleasant and easy, is something that any ambitious firm should strive to find. It is certainly what made my time at Prep Zone most memorable, and led me to turning to him for outside help in the three companies I have worked at since. For all these reasons and more, I treasure my acquaintance with Vrijen Attawar, and highly recommend him in his future endeavors.

Vrijen was a student in my Communications Ethics Law and Regulation class at NYU’s School of Professional Studies in the 2019 Fall semester.  Vrijen is very good at honing and articulating a succinct and impactful message.  He is very personable and insightful.  He definitely is at ease discussing and dissecting difficult subject matters, and complex issues.  Vrijen is also an excellent writer, who is creative and persuasive.  Additionally, he was well-prepared and well-versed in the course subject matter and assigned readings, which showed to me that he is a self-starter who possesses a great work ethic.  He worked well in teams for group exercises, and was always courteous and respectful toward differing opinions.  His calm demeanor and outstanding presentation skills in front of the class made him stand out in class.  I happily and without reservation would recommend Vrijen for positions in the public affairs and public communications fields because the skills he demonstrated in class will serve future employers well.  In short, he is very bright and capable, and possesses both the desire and ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment.

My name is Logan Henderson Currie—current CEO & co-founder of The Keys Admissions. I have worked with Vrijen since 2015 and founded my current business with him in 2017. I can confidently say that Vrijen Attawar combines dedication, expertise, and sheer effort to a level I have never seen during my nine years in the private education industry.   Vrijen was an extremely versatile teammate and co-founder: whether it was a partnership negotiation, client sales meeting, or presentation to our private equity management, he was able to handle it all. I could throw him into any situation, confident in the knowledge he could turn it to his—and our—favor. His skill as a communicator is perhaps his greatest attribute; Vrijen is an exceptionally articulate and thoughtful individual who can translate his ideas into speech or writing with ease.   I decided to hire him in 2015 because I was impressed by his candor, intelligence, and attitude, and I count it as one of the best decisions I’ve made. He is undoubtedly a superlative teammate, employee, and person. His desire to excel is palpable, and he unquestionably possesses the intellectual capacity, focus, and dedication to achieve anything to which he sets his mind.   I cannot recommend Vrijen more as a potential employee and collaborator. I invite you to contact me should you have any further questions about him.  Sincerely,  Logan Currie  University of Pennsylvania, Magna Cum Laude Graduate of the Class of 2009  loganhc@gmail.com // logan@thekeysadmissions.org
